channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House Republicans brush off Stefanik's Trump admin upheaval amid high-stakes Florida race
item.description: House Republican leaders are dismissing speculation that they have been anxious over three GOP-favored congressional districts in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oliver Stone to testify at JFK files House hearing
item.description: Filmmaker Oliver Stone will be a witness at a congressional hearing about the release of materials pertaining to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump to roll out new tariffs that he promises will free the US from foreign goods
item.description: President Donald Trump says Wednesday will be “Liberation Day” — when he plans to roll out a set of tariffs he promises will free the United States from foreign goods.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk-funded political group spends big and goes door to door in the Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: Elon Musk and his political operatives have tried to shape the closely watched Wisconsin Supreme Court race in ways that are not immediately obvious but could be critical to Tuesday’s outcome.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Wisconsin Supreme Court vote is getting national attention and millions from Musk
item.description: The court could face cases on abortion, congressional maps and labor unions. With Tesla suing the state, Elon Musk is backing the conservative candidate who’s received President Trump’s endorsement.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: Store, harvest, fix: How Texas can save its water supply
item.description: State lawmakers are poised to devote billions to save the state’s water supply. These are some of the ways the state could spend the money.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandra Martinez And Jayme Lozano Carver, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term as president
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that “I’m not joking” about trying to serve a third term, the clearest indication he is considering ways to breach a constitutional barrier against continuing to lead the country after his second term ends at the beginning of 2029.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Historic tree to be cut down at the White House over safety concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump says a tree that’s nearly two centuries old will be removed from the White House grounds because it’s deteriorating.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From Seattle to Miami, anti-Musk protesters gather at hundreds of Tesla locations
item.description: At a rally in Los Angeles, 65-year-old Phil Ansell said he wanted to participate because "I want to do everything possible to protect democracy in this country.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he is 'pissed off' with Putin over lack of peace progress: report
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is "very angry" at Russian President Vladimir Putin for hampering peace talks with new criticisms of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk hands out $1 million payments after Wisconsin Supreme Court declines request to stop him
item.description: Elon Musk has given out $1 million checks to two Wisconsin voters, declaring them spokespeople for his political group.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts, vying to exploit the GOP’s advised moratorium on the events to make inroads with frustrated voters, pick up battleground seats, and flip control of the House in next year’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, received email notices of their mass firing, the latest step in the Trump administration's government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Critics see Trump attacks on the 'Black Smithsonian' as an effort to sanitize racism in US history
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump's order accusing the Smithsonian Institution of not reflecting American history notes correctly that the country's Founding Fathers declared that “all men are created equal.” But it doesn't mention that the founders enshrined slavery into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'resuscitated’ Canada’s Liberal Party: Stirewalt
item.description: Stirewalt believes Trump “tipped over” Canadian politics by giving Canadians a “common enemy” in Trump and U.S. trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
